faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: VEYe is a community safety app that helps you stay informed about alerts and danger zones near you in Haiti. You can view incidents on a map, report what you see, and receive push notifications for nearby alerts.
faq.q2: How do I report an incident?
faq.a2: Tap the + button in the center of the bottom bar, then select the type (Kidnapping, Missing person, Shooting, Dangerous zone, or Suspicious activity). Add a description and optional photos. Your location is auto-detected. You can report anonymously.
faq.q3: What is the Alert Radius vs Notification Radius?
faq.a3: Alert Radius controls how far from your location alerts and danger zones are shown on the map (default 15 km). Notification Radius controls how far alerts must be from you to trigger push notifications (default 25 km). You can customize both in Profile.
faq.q4: How do I change the language?
faq.a4: Go to Profile, tap Language, and choose English, Français, or Kreyòl. The app will update immediately.
faq.a5: Yes. You can enable "Report Anonymously" when submitting. Even without that option, VEYe does not display your identity publicly. Reports are verified by the team before being shared.
faq.q6: What do the alert colors mean?
faq.a6: Red = Active kidnapping or extreme danger. Orange = Dangerous zone or shooting. Yellow = Suspicious activity or missing person. Green = Safe or released. Blue = General information.
faq.q7: Why does VEYe need my location?
faq.a7: Location is used to show you relevant alerts and danger zones near you, and to send push notifications only for incidents within your notification radius. It also helps when you report an incident so we know where it occurred.
faq.q8: How can I share an alert with family or friends?
faq.a8: Open the alert details and tap "Share Alert." You can also share the VEYe app from Profile > Share VEYe so others can stay safe too.

permissions.locationMessage: VEYe needs your location to show nearby alerts and keep you safe.
